3GPP_Reference.Title: NG-RAN; Architecture description, NG-RAN; F1 Application Protocol (F1AP)

Feature_Description: This procedure is used for the case when the UE moves from one gNB-DU to another gNB-DU within the same gNB-CU during NR operation for LTM (LTM handover).
Feature_Objectives: To enable the gNB-CU to prepare and send an F1AP 'UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST' message to a candidate gNB-DU for LTM configuration.
To allow the candidate gNB-DU to respond with an F1AP 'UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE' message, indicating acceptance and providing lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell.
To facilitate the establishment of UE context at the candidate gNB-DU for Inter-gNB-DU LTM handover.
Feature_Implementation_Steps: 1. The UE sends a MeasurementReport message (L3 measurement result) to the source gNB-DU containing measurements of neighbouring cells. The source gNB-DU sends an UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER message conveying the received MeasurementReport message to the gNB-CU.
2. The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration.

3. The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell, containing one candidate cell ID and the CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM. The gNB-CU may provide the LTM configuration ID mapping list to the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request PRACH resources from the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request the candidate gNB-DU(s) to provide the lower layer configuration for the purpose of generating the reference configuration or provide the lower layer part of the reference configuration to the candidate gNB-DU(s).

4. If the candidate gNB-DU accepts the request of LTM configuration, it responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message including the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell.
Procedure_Implementation_Steps: 1. The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration.

2. The gNB-CU sends a UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST message to the candidate gNB-DU(s) for each candidate cell, containing one candidate cell ID and the CSI resource configuration for subsequent LTM. The gNB-CU may provide the LTM configuration ID mapping list to the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request PRACH resources from the candidate gNB-DU(s). The gNB-CU may request the candidate gNB-DU(s) to provide the lower layer configuration for the purpose of generating the reference configuration or provide the lower layer part of the reference configuration to the candidate gNB-DU(s).

3. If the candidate gNB-DU accepts the request of LTM configuration, it responds with a UE CONTEXT SETUP RESPONSE message including the generated lower layer RRC configurations for the accepted target candidate cell.

Information_Elements.IE_Definition:
UECONTEXTSETUPREQUESTIEs ::= SEQUENCE {
    gNB-CU-UE-F1AP-ID                  GNB-CU-UE-F1AP-ID,
    gNB-DU-UE-F1AP-ID                  GNB-DU-UE-F1AP-ID,
    candidateCell-ID                   CandidateCell-ID,
    lTM-ConfigurationID-MappingList   LTM-ConfigurationID-MappingList OPTIONAL,
    cSI-ResourceConfig                 CSI-ResourceConfig OPTIONAL,
    pRACH-Resource                    
}

Knowledge_Hints: 10 (ts_138473v180600p): Handling of unknown, unforeseen and erroneous protocol data
